title: ludwigia peploides subsp. glabrescens
intro: Ludwigia peploides subsp. glabrescens is a subspecies of aquatic plant, commonly known as creeping water-willow or water primrose. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Ludwigia peploides subsp. glabrescens is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems are sometimes dried and used in capsule or powder form.
